# Panno.ai Skills

> Curated Claude Code agent skills by Panno.ai. Each skill is a self-contained
> folder with a SKILL.md and optional scripts, references, and assets.

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/panno-ai/skills
- License: MIT
- Install all skills: npx skills add panno-ai/skills
- Install one skill:  npx skills add panno-ai/skills --skill <name>

## Skills

### llm-resource-usage

Analyzes LLM/AI token usage across local coding agents (Claude Code, OpenClaw,
Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Goose, Amp, etc.), enriches it with environmental
impact data (energy, CO₂, water, datacenter location), and produces a visual
HTML infographic and narrative report.

- Path: skills/llm-resource-usage/
- SKILL.md: skills/llm-resource-usage/SKILL.md
- Requires: Python 3.8+, optional tokscale CLI
- Tags: analytics, llm-usage, environment, carbon, reporting, tokens, cost
- Homepage: https://github.com/panno-ai/skills/tree/main/skills/llm-resource-usage

## Skill Format

Each skill follows the agentskills.io open standard:

- SKILL.md — YAML frontmatter (name, description, license, compatibility, metadata) + markdown instructions
- scripts/  — executable Python/Node scripts invoked by the skill
- references/ — static JSON/data loaded into context on demand
- assets/    — files used in output (not committed)

## Adding a Skill

1. Copy skills/template/ to skills/<your-skill-name>/
2. Fill in SKILL.md frontmatter and write instructions
3. Add scripts to scripts/, static data to references/
4. Open a PR against main

## Optional Metadata Fields (agentskills.io spec)

- license        — short license name
- compatibility  — environment requirements
- metadata.author, metadata.version, metadata.tags, metadata.homepage
- metadata.openclaw.requires.bins — for ClawHub compatibility
